subject: ap-chemistry
title: Chemical Kinetics & Rate Laws
stimulus:
Answer the following questions about the isomers fulminic acid and isocyanic acid.
Two possible Lewis electron-dot diagrams for fulminic acid, HCNO, are shown below.

parts.prompt:
Explain why the diagram on the left is the better representation for the bonding in fulminic acid. Justify your
choice based on formal charges.
Fulminic acid can convert to isocyanic acid according to the equation below.
HCNO(g) HNCO(g)
fulminic acid isocyanic acid
Fulminic Acid Isocyanic Acid

scoring_points.description: a correct assignment of formal charges in the two diagrams

required_elements: a correct assignment of formal charges in the two diagrams

parts.prompt:

equilibrium at 298 K? Justify your answer in terms of thermodynamic favorability and the equilibrium
constant.
The ammonium salt of isocyanic acid is a product of the decomposition of urea, CO(NH ) , represented below.
